Accessibility

We are passionate about accessiility and meet WCAG 2.1 AA standard because everyone deserves access to information that will help them.

Last updated: May 2026

No Spoons Wasted is built for and by someone who understands firsthand that access matters. We are committed to making this site usable for everyone, regardless of ability or the assistive technology they rely on.

Our commitment

We aim to meet the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 Level AA standard across all pages of this site. These guidelines explain how to make web content more accessible to people with disabilities, including those with visual, auditory, physical, cognitive, and neurological conditions.

How we test

We actively test this site for accessibility using:

  • WAVE (Web Accessibility Evaluation Tool) — checks for errors, contrast issues, and structural problems
  • SilkTide — automated accessibility scanning across the full site

We test regularly and address issues as they are identified. We treat accessibility as an ongoing practice, not a one-time checkbox.

Known limitations

No site is perfect. If you encounter something that creates a barrier for you, we want to know about it. We will not assume something is accessible just because our tools did not flag it.

What we’ve done

To meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards, we have:

  • Ensured all color combinations meet minimum contrast ratios
  • Added descriptive alt text to images
  • Used proper heading structure throughout
  • Made sure the site is navigable by keyboard
  • Used semantic HTML so screen readers can interpret content correctly
  • Ensured links have descriptive text rather than generic “click here” labels

Feedback and contact

If you experience an accessibility barrier on this site, please let us know using our contact form. Tell us:

  • The page where you encountered the issue
  • What you were trying to do
  • What assistive technology you were using, if any

We will respond within 10 business days and do our best to provide the information or functionality you need in an accessible format while we work on a fix.

We take all accessibility feedback seriously. Your experience using this site matters to us.

Formal complaints

If you are not satisfied with our response, you may contact the Web Accessibility Initiative (WAI) at the W3C for further guidance: w3.org/WAI

Technical information

This site is built with Jekyll and hosted on SiteGround. It is designed to be compatible with the following assistive technologies:

  • Screen readers including NVDA, JAWS, and VoiceOver
  • Keyboard-only navigation
  • Browser zoom up to 200% without loss of content or functionality
  • High contrast mode

Contact

Questions about accessibility on this site? We welcome the conversation.

Use our contact form or email us directly.

No Spoons Wasted

No Spoons Wasted is a blog for people who live with low energy. We're here with recipes, practical life hacks, and honest product recommendations for the days when just existing takes everything you've got.
